claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, I'll need to gather some information about your situation. Let me begin with a few questions.

**First, how many children are you responsible for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wondering if I'm eligible. 

I have one child - Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, there are specific rules that apply.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** For example, is Alex attending university, college, or a government-approved training programme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's doing his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A-levels at sixth form college count as approved education, which is good news.

**Are you living with Alex, or is there a different arrangement regarding where Alex lives?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I have a clearer picture of your situation.

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) earning over £60,000 per year individually?** This is important because there's a tax charge that applies to Child Benefit if either of you has an individual income above this threshold, though you would still be eligible to claim.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Just to confirm a couple of final details:

**Are you living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility.

Based on the information you've provided, let me explain your situation:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 18 years old
- Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's upkeep
- Your income is below £60,000

According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. However, if your child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if they're under 20 and stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 18, still in approved education (A-levels), and living with you, you meet all the criteria for eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Excellent news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since Alex is 18 but still in approved education (A-levels), you qualify to continue receiving Child Benefit for them. The key requirements are all met:
- Alex is under 20 and in approved education
- You live with Alex and are responsible for their upkeep
- You live in the UK

If you'd like to proceed with a claim, you can apply through the official government website or contact HMRC for guidance on how to claim.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives with Alex in the UK, and their income is below £60,000. According to the rules, Child Benefit normally continues for children aged 16 or over if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. Alex meets all these criteria.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex is 18, they are still under 20 and in approved education, which qualifies them for continued Child Benefit eligibility. The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for their upkeep, and lives in the UK, meeting all the necessary conditions.">
